summary: FPV drones continue to dominate the battlefield, with multiple incidents showing their use against infantry, vehicles, and even armored systems. In Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine's 225th Assault Regiment repelled a Russian motorcycle assault using FPV drones, while elsewhere, drones struck two Russian soldiers on motorcycles and intercepted a Russian soldier attempting to fake surrender. A Russian TOS-1A MLRS was destroyed in a coordinated strike using a Bulava loitering munition followed by FPV UAVs. The Rubicon Center reported over 24,000 enemy target destructions, with unmanned systems (UAVs and reconnaissance complexes) making up 36.7% of kills. Ukrainian forces captured a Ukrainian Armed Forces "Shark-M" UAV after its parachute deployed during a kinetic interception by a Russian "Yolka" air defense drone in the Krasny Liman area.
